title: lyogavin/airllm
friendlyTitle: AirLLM: run bigger models on smaller GPUs
summary: AirLLM is a project for running very large language models with much lower GPU memory requirements, including claims of 70B inference on a single 4GB GPU and 405B-class loading on 8GB VRAM. It uses layer-wise loading and optional compression to reduce memory pressure.
friendlySummary: AirLLM helps very large AI models run with less GPU memory than usual.
whyItMatters: Use it to explore large-model inference on constrained hardware, understand memory-saving inference techniques or prototype local experiments when full-GPU hosting is too expensive.
friendlyWhyItMatters: It is useful for people experimenting with large models who do not have expensive GPU servers.
